entry.title: Re: SAP HANA Cloud Upgrade (Version 2026.2.6 – QRC 1/2026)
entry.content:
Dear Saikumar33

Merges are triggered either by another process implicitly (log replay, recovery) or through a merge decision function at regular intervals every 60s and might trigger a merge activity if certain thresholds are exceeded, or not. Shorter merge intervals would normally mean there is increased DML activity happening.

With QRC2 2025, the merge thread parallelism (how many table columns are merged at the same time) is 0, and therefore directly controlled by the instance size. 
If you experience long running merges as well, please consider upsizing. 

Neither the interval nor the decision function can be changed by customers, as these are optimized and maintained by SAP.

If you are interested in delta merges, to an extent you may reference the KBA 2057046 - FAQ: SAP HANA Delta Merges 
Be aware that most of the information there is for HANA Platform Edition, with some references to QRC changes in SAP HANA Cloud. The principle of the mechanism is almost the same.

If you are experiencing unexplainably short merge intervals, please reach out to SAP Support directly through a support case to troubleshoot your specific issue.

Please find specific (patch-) release information through the following resources: 

Patches and fixes (Patch releases): 
3716297 - SAP HANA Cloud 2026 QRC1 Patch Information

New features in SAP HANA Cloud (QRC release): 
https://help.sap.com/whats-new/2495b34492334456a49084831c2bea4e?Component=SAP+HANA+Cloud&Version=2026.2+(QRC+1/2026)

Kind regards, 
Oliver

entry.title: Valuation Class Change – Posting Period Restriction and Financial Impact Clarification
entry.content:
Description:

We are performing a mass valuation class change for materials with existing stock.

Current scenario:

- Stock is written off at the end of the period (e.g., April 30)
- Valuation class is updated
- System does not allow posting stock back in the same period after valuation class change
- Error received: "Back-posting only possible with same valuation class"

As a result:

- We are required to post stock back in the next period (e.g., May 1)
- This creates a timing difference where:
 - Inventory is reduced in April
 - Inventory is restored in May
- Finance reports show consumption in April and discrepancies in month-end closing

Questions:
1. Is there any supported approach to complete write-off and write-on within the same posting period when valuation class changes?
2. Are there SAP-recommended best practices to minimize financial reporting impact in this scenario?

I would appreciate confirmation and any recommended approach for handling this process in a way that aligns with financial reporting requirements.

entry.title: Re: SAP Note 3694507 – Package Information Missing for Ma...
entry.content:
3694507 - [TDF SP20] Note 018: EFD-Reinf - Improvements for error handling of certificate errors - SAP for Me

The manual instruction says:

1. CREATE THE NEW ERROR MESSAGE
1.1 Go to t-code SE91.
1.2 Enter message classe: “/TMF/MF” and click on Change button.

So, the package is automatically the one of the existing message class (you must not enter a Z package).

If anything else happens, it means that there's something wrong in your installation, or the way you have installed the SAP notes or in this SAP note itself, so either diagnose by yourself or contact SAP Support.

entry.title: Re: The cost center cannot be changed in the WBS element...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Once a project is created it is no longer possible to edit the Company Code. Then once there are postings on a WBS/Project it is no longer possible to edit the Cost Center. As this is the current standard behavior please consider voting on Improvement Request https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/329790 if you would like to see this behavior changed in future releases.

Best regards, 

Tiago 
SAP Technical Support

entry.title: Re: How to check the memory usage of a specific table in...
entry.content:
Hi, 

You can see the memory consumption from different areas of the table via the M_CS_TABLES view also.

Example :

SELECT  TABLE_NAME, PART_ID, MEMORY_SIZE_IN_MAIN, MEMORY_SIZE_IN_TOTAL, MEMORY_SIZE_IN_DELTA, RECORD_COUNT, RAW_RECORD_COUNT_IN_MAIN, RAW_RECORD_COUNT_IN_DELTA,
FROM M_CS_TABLES WHERE TABLE_NAME = '<table name>'

Kind Regards
Simon

entry.title: Avoid create sales order from certain sales office and invoice from certain business area
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community

Sales Order creation in VA01

I have Sales office1, Sales office2 and Sales office3 in the same sales organization.

Sales office1 and Sales office2 could create also sales orders in Sales office3. However, Sales office3 shouldn't create the sales order in the rest of the Sales offices.

We have a role that has V_VBAK_VKO with the sales organization and channel distribution.

We have try to add the sales office to block in VA01, but it hasn´t worked. It seems that it isn´t work in sales office level. Is it this correct or is there a way it can work?

I've seen the OSS note 3046057 - Authorization check regarding sales office and sales group in sales document. This note have an example to create a z authorization object. Can´t we manage it by authorizations in roles, without ABAP? It's just I wanted to know if there weren't any other way apart from using user-exit.

Invoice creation in VF01 and VF04

We have the same problem at invoice creation VF01 and VF04 transaction, how to manage the authorizations in roles to filter by business area? I have business area1 and 2 that can see BA3, but BA3 shouldn´t see BA1 and BA2. We are using V_VBRK_VKO. Is there a way to manage by standard authorization objects?

 

Kindly ask for your advice and thank you in advance.

Best regards

entry.title: HTML5 (Custom Fiori) app fails with 500 (token exchange) in TST only – works in DEV & ACC landscape
entry.content:
We have a custom HTML5 app deployed to the HTML5 Application Repository and launched via managed Launchpad / Work Zone.

  •  DEV – works
  • ACC – works
  • TST – fails

Same MTA, same UI artifacts, same configuration pattern across all landscapes.

Problem (TST only)

  • /index.html
  • /manifest.json
  • /Component-preload.js

All return:
HTTP 500 – Internal Server Error Failed to exchange login token by an XSUAA token Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'url') identityzone undefined / clientid undefined

This happens for all users and occurs before any backend (CAP) call.

What is already verified

  1. HTML5 app is correctly uploaded and visible via cf html5-list
  2. Single app ID
  3. public visibility
  4. HTML5 repository destination exists and matches ACC:
  • sap.cloud.service
  • uri / content_endpoint
  • app_host_id
  • xsappname
  • OAuth2ClientCredentials
  • HTML5 repo host service key contains valid UAA data
  • Work Zone content provider updated
  • Same issue when launching via HTML5 Applications (not Work Zone specific)
  • Other HTML5 apps run fine in the same TST subaccount

Key observation
Since static files fail, this looks like a managed approuter / Launchpad runtime token‑exchange failure, not a CAP or UI build issue.

Has anyone seen a case where:

  • HTML5 repo content is present and public
  • Repository destination & service key are correct
  • DEV/ACC work
  • but one landscape fails with 500 during managed runtime token exchange?

Is this a known:

  • Launchpad runtime issue?
  • HTML5 repository runtime issue?
  • Internal managed destination / UAA resolution issue?
  • Any pointers, SAP Notes, or similar experience would be very helpful.

Thanks in advance 

Regards,
Raghav

entry.title: SAP Learning Hub Gets Smarter: AI-Assisted Learning Is Here
entry.content:
Building real SAP skills just got faster, more flexible, and more intelligent. SAP Learning Hub now brings AI-assisted learning directly into your experience, so you can learn, practice, and prepare for certification in a way that mirrors how SAP work actually gets done. 

At the heart of it is AI that meets you where you are. As a customer or partner using SAP Learning Hub, you can ask a question in natural language, in any of 80+ languages, and get an immediate, trusted answer drawn straight from SAP Learning Hub content. Prefer to learn through a mind map, podcast, or interactive Q&A? AI-supported formats adapt to how you learn best, making it easier to absorb and retain what matters.  Whatever your learning style, there is a format that works for you. 

The real impact shows up in practice. While working directly in SAP systems, you now have AI guidance on hand to help you explore, troubleshoot, and reinforce understanding in the flow of real work, not just in theory. It's the kind of support that builds genuine confidence, not just familiarity. 

Preparing for SAP Certification? Your preparation is now more closely aligned to performance-based exams, with AI-supported tools that reflect real project scenarios. AI-powered simulations and avatars give you a more realistic, engaging space to build confidence before stepping into live customer environments, so that when it matters, you’re ready.  

This is upskilling through SAP Learning Hub, designed for the way work is done today.  

Watch the feature overview video here and explore everything SAP Learning Hub has to offer: SAP Learning Hub | Your Online E-Learning Enablement Platform

entry.title: RBP Permissions have been affected with the new people profile.
entry.content:
The Role based permissions have been affected with the New People profile. It has made the information available to all the employees irrespective of the old permissions. Ideally, they should have taken care of the old permissions.

Also, when you wish to select the RBP in the Configure Latest People profile, it automatically selects that permission for all the roles, it doesn't even show which groups are getting affected.

It is really risky and there is no clarity on the configuration and the roles which are being impacted via the upgrade to Employee Profile.

As per the information on help.sap.com, RBPs were supposed to remain the same, but it seems like a lot has been impacted with the upgrade of people profile. Not at all happy with this.

entry.title: Re: How to Take SAP’s New Performance-Based Certification Exams
entry.content:
in the SuccessFactors's practical system-based exams, we are not able to find a scoring strategy or rubric to help us understand the results of our exams.  Previously, we received a breakdown of the percent questions correct by area.  Now, we only get the overall score.  We have opened a ticket with SAP and have been told that we will not receive a detailed breakdown of what was right/wrong.  I understand this logic - can anyone answer a basic question:  does a task have to be 100% correct or is there partial credit assigned?  In the test I took, there are tasks that have 1 main part, and there are those tasks that are multi-part.  Is the AI reviewing the coding of our templates or system to confirm final configuration for the whole task?  Is there partial credit?

It is difficult to study to retake an exam if you don't know at least what area was not 100%, but I understand the logic.  In a practical exam, like PM//GM/Dev/Calibration, perhaps at the very least, a specific module score breakdown would give us a learning path forward.

Thank you,
TKramer

entry.title: Integración de los vales de despensa al salario Diario integrado fijo
entry.content:
Tengo un tema acerca de la integración de los vales de despensa al salario diario Integrado Fijo, que espero que me puedan apoyar al respecto.

Situación Actual

Los vales de despensa para los empleados semanales, quincenales y mensuales están clasificados como ingreso variable, es decir, para la integración de salarios, este ingreso está considerado como Salario diario variable

Se requiere integrar los vales de despensa al salario diario fijo, considerando que éstos son depositados (vía tarjeta de vales de despensa) de la siguiente manera:

 

El registro de los vales de despensa:

Se realiza a través del infotipo 0377

Aplica para:

Empleados Semanales      - Se depositan cada semana

Empleados Quincenales    - Se depositan en la 2a quincena del mes

Empleados Mensuales       - Se depositan 1 vez al mes

Objetivo:

Conocer si existe la forma de implementar lo anteriormente descrito de manera estándar.

De no existir una implementación estándar (con base a lo descrito anteriormente), me gustaría conocer el procedimiento para implementar solución requerida.

¿Será que hay algo más que necesites saber, y que no haya incluido para poder apoyarme en la posible solución de implementación?

De antemano agradezco el apoyo.

entry.title: Usage of DDIC Tables in SAP Cloud – CDS Views / APIs Availability
entry.content:
Hi,

 

We are working in a SAP Cloud environment (SAP HANA Cloud / S/4HANA Cloud) and need to consume data that is currently available via certain DDIC tables.

As direct access to DDIC tables is not allowed or recommended in SAP Cloud, we would like your guidance on cloud‑compliant options:

  • Are there any released and approved standard CDS views that provide the same data as these DDIC tables?
  • If CDS views are not available, is there any released cloud API (OData / REST / RAP) that can be used to consume this data?
  • Are these tables restricted or not released for cloud usage, as per SAP’s clean core and extensibility guidelines?
  • If neither CDS views nor APIs exist, what is the recommended approach in the cloud (for example, using released interfaces, custom CDS views based on released objects, or BTP‑based data extraction)?

Our objective is to follow SAP‑recommended, cloud‑compliant, upgrade‑safe, and clean‑core principles, avoiding any direct dependency on DDIC tables.

Please let us know if further details are required from our side.

DD02T
DD03L
DD04T
DD07T

entry.title: SAP Successpläne - Wie eine Boxencrew für Ihre Cloud-Transformation
entry.content:
Dieser Blogbeitrag fasst ein Webinar am 14.04.2026 von Simone Ruprechter und Johannes Schelble zusammen. Das Webinar ist Teil einer Reihe zu den neuen Success Plänen.

SAP hat sein Service- und Support-Portfolio vereinfacht und überarbeitet. Die neuen „Success Plans" ersetzen bisherige Modelle und sollen Kunden nicht nur beim Go-Live, sondern kontinuierlich dabei unterstützen, den vollen Nutzen aus ihren Cloud-Investitionen zu ziehen.  

Warum SAP das Service Portfolio jetzt umbaut

SAP reagiert mit den Success Plans auf drei Entwicklungen:

  • Die Cloud-Transformation erfordert dauerhafte Begleitung statt punktueller Projektunterstützung.
  • Kunden wünschen sich einen einheitlichen Ansatz über alle SAP-Lösungen hinweg.
  • Und das wirtschaftliche Umfeld verlangt agile Reaktionsfähigkeit – nicht erst dann, wenn Probleme auftauchen.

Kundenfeedback stand laut Ruprechter im Zentrum des Redesigns. Die Kernforderungen: einfachere Zusammenarbeit mit SAP Services, proaktives Handeln statt reaktivem Support und ein konsistentes Modell über die gesamte SAP-Landschaft.

Die Formel-1-Analogie: Strategie schlägt Technik

Ruprechter eröffnete mit einer Parallele zur Formel 1: Das Team Brawn GP gewann 2009 die Konstrukteursweltmeisterschaft – nicht mit dem besten Auto, sondern mit der besten Strategie. Fehlerlose Boxenstopps, ausgewertete Telemetriedaten und die Fähigkeit, in Echtzeit auf veränderte Bedingungen zu reagieren, machten den Unterschied. Die Success Plans sollen genau diese Rolle für SAP-Kunden übernehmen: das strategische Team im Hintergrund.

Drei Stufen, aufeinander aufbauend

Die Success Plans gliedern sich in drei Ebenen, die jeweils auf der vorherigen aufbauen:

Foundational Success Plan

Der Foundational Plan ist in jeder Cloud-Subskription enthalten. Er bietet Self-Services: Learning Journeys, Zugriff auf die SAP Model Company, SLAs und umfangreichen Content. Kunden erschließen sich Inhalte viele vielen Self-Services – eine solide Basis, jedoch ohne persönliche Begleitung.

Advanced Success Plan

Der Advanced Plan ergänzt den Foundational Plan um drei zentrale Komponenten:

  • Innovation and Adoption Guidance: SAP-Experten zeigen auf, welche Funktionalitäten und Innovationen – etwa KI-gestützte Prozesse – für den konkreten Anwendungsfall relevant sind. Sie bereiten Entscheidungsgrundlagen auf und helfen bei der Bewertung neuer Releases.

  • Activation and Optimization Services: Hands-on-Unterstützung am System. Experten helfen, neue Funktionalitäten zu aktivieren, unnötiges Customizing zu identifizieren und Prozesse zu optimieren.

  • KI-gestütztes Monitoring: Ein Tool überwacht definierte KPIs, erkennt Abweichungen und schlägt konkrete Maßnahmen vor, um wieder in den Normbereich zu kommen.

Entscheidend: Diese Services stehen unlimitiert zur Verfügung. Kunden müssen keine einzelnen Beratertage buchen, sondern können die Leistungen abrufen, wann immer Bedarf besteht.

Max Success Plan

Der Max Plan richtet sich an Unternehmen mit komplexeren Transformationsszenarien. Er baut auf dem Advanced Plan auf und bringt zusätzlich:

  • Einen Success Plan Manager – eine dedizierte Ansprechperson, die vorausschauend plant und durch unvorhergesehene Herausforderungen navigiert. Die Rolle entwickelt sich aus dem bisherigen Technical Quality Manager (TQM) der Premium-Engagement-Services.
  • Transformation Advisory Services für strategische Begleitung in komplexen Projekten
  • Prototyp Development Services, etwa für KI-Use-Cases
  • Cross-Solution Services für End-to-End-Prozessoptimierung über Lösungsgrenzen hinweg (z. B. ERP zu SuccessFactors)
  • Support Case Management Assistance mit einem dedizierten Case Manager in kritischen Phasen

Im Max Plan können auch individualisierte Systemausprägungen betrachtet und Wege zurück zum Standard aufgezeigt werden.

Flexibilität als Designprinzip

Ein zentraler Aspekt ist, daß Kunden nicht denselben Success Plan über ihre gesamte Systemlandschaft hinweg anwenden müssen. Für ein kritisches ERP-Projekt kann der Max Plan gelten, während für eine CX-Lösung der Foundational Plan ausreichen kann. Diese Wahlfreiheit erlaubt es, Budgets gezielt dort einzusetzen, wo der größte Bedarf bzw. Hebel liegt.

Die Plans sind Co-termed mit der jeweiligen Cloud-Subskription. Der Foundational Plan ist immer inkludiert, Advanced und Max stellen einen Uplift dar. Bei größeren Software-Landschaften gibt es die Möglichkeit, mit Rabatten zu arbeiten.

KI-Strategie auf jeder Stufe

Auch das Thema Business AI ist in den Success Plans verankert – abgestuft nach Tiefe. 

Vom reaktiven Support zur proaktiven Partnerschaft

Der Paradigmenwechsel, den die Success Plans markieren ist folgender: Weg vom Support, der bei Problemen hilft, hin zu einer vorausschauenden Begleitung. SAP-Experten weisen proaktiv auf ungenutztes Potenzial hin, ordnen kommende Innovationen ein und helfen, Optimierungspotenziale zu erkennen – bevor Probleme entstehen.

 

Kern-Aussagen aus dem Webinar

„Brawn GP gewann nicht wegen des stärksten Motors. Sie gewannen, weil jedes System, jede Person, alle Prozesse auf ein Ziel ausgerichtet waren: Performance, Effizienz und kontinuierliche Innovation."
Simone Ruprechter

„Wir sind Ihre Boxencrew. Wir helfen Ihnen, die Strategie umzusetzen. Wir sind Ihr Team im Background."
Simone Ruprechter

„Unsere Aufgabe ist es, für Sie da zu sein, wann immer Sie uns brauchen – damit Sie in dem sich verändernden Umfeld die bestmöglichen Entscheidungen treffen und die Software bestmöglich einsetzen."
Simone Ruprechter

Praktische Tipps für die Umsetzung

  1. Bestandsaufnahme machen: Prüfen Sie, wo Ihre Lösungen in der Transformation stehen – am Anfang, mittendrin oder in der Optimierung. Je nach Phase eignet sich ein anderer Success Plan.

  2. Differenziert wählen: Nutzen Sie die Möglichkeit, pro Lösung unterschiedliche Plan-Stufen zu wählen. Kritische Projekte verdienen den Max Plan, stabile Systeme kommen mit Foundational aus.

  3. Self-Services im Foundational Plan nicht unterschätzen: Learning Journeys und die Model Company bieten bereits substanziellen Mehrwert – vorausgesetzt, man nutzt sie aktiv.

  4. KI-Potenziale früh evaluieren: Auch ohne Max Plan lassen sich im Advanced Plan bereits KI-Use-Cases identifizieren und aktivieren.

  5. Proaktive Services einfordern: Im Advanced und Max Plan stehen Ihnen Experten zur Verfügung. Warten Sie nicht auf Probleme, sondern nutzen Sie die Advisory-Leistungen, um Optimierungspotenziale vorab zu identifizieren.

  6. SAP direkt ansprechen: Für eine individuelle Empfehlung, welcher Plan-Mix zu Ihrer Landschaft passt, empfiehlt SAP den direkten Kontakt.

Resümee

SAP positioniert die neuen Success Plans als strategische Partnerschaft statt als klassischen Support-Vertrag. Das dreistufige Modell – Foundational, Advanced, Max – gibt Kunden die Flexibilität, den Grad der Unterstützung pro Lösung selbst zu bestimmen. Der Kern des neuen Ansatzes: proaktive, vorausschauende Begleitung über den gesamten Lebenszyklus der Cloud-Lösung, nicht nur als Absicherung im Projekt. Wer komplexe Transformationsszenarien vor sich hat, findet im Max Plan mit dediziertem Success Plan Manager und Cross-Solution Services die intensivste Betreuung.

Dies war der Überblick - weitere Webinare folgen. Ich verlinke alle hier im Blog SAP Success Pläne - Der Guide zum neuen SAP Support und Service Modell 2026.

 

FAQ

Was ist der Unterschied zwischen den drei Success-Plan-Stufen?

Die drei Stufen – Foundational, Advanced und Max – bauen aufeinander auf. Foundational bietet Self-Services wie Learning Journeys und SLAs. Advanced ergänzt dies um persönliche Beratung, Aktivierungs- und Optimierungsservices sowie KI-gestütztes Monitoring. Max fügt einen dedizierten Success Plan Manager, Transformation Advisory, Prototyp-Entwicklung und Cross-Solution-Services hinzu.

Ist der Foundational Plan automatisch enthalten?

Ja. Der Foundational Success Plan ist automatisch in den Cloud-Lizenzen enthalten. Die weiteren Pläne (Advanced und Max) sind situativ empfohlen, jedoch nicht verpflichtend.

Sind die Success Plans verpflichtende Bestandteile der Cloud-Transformation?

Nein. Der Foundational Plan ist automatisch in der Cloud-Subskription enthalten. Advanced und Max sind optionale Erweiterungen, die situativ empfohlen werden, aber nicht verpflichtend sind.

Muss ich für alle meine SAP-Lösungen denselben Plan wählen?

Nein. Sie können pro Lösung unterschiedliche Plan-Stufen wählen – etwa Max für ein kritisches ERP-Projekt und Foundational für eine CX-Lösung ohne akuten Bedarf.

Ich habe derzeit ActiveAttention – kann ich auf die neuen Success Plans wechseln?

Ja, es gibt Möglichkeiten zu wechseln. SAP empfiehlt, für die konkreten Details mit dem zuständigen TQM Kontakt aufzunehmen.

Was passiert mit den bisherigen Premium-Engagement-Services?

Die Premium-Engagement-Services fließen in den Max Success Plan ein. Die Rolle des Technical Quality Managers (TQM) entwickelt sich zum Success Plan Manager weiter.

Gelten die Success Plans auch für On-Premise-Systeme?

Die Success Plans sind für Cloud-Systeme und solche, die perspektivisch in die Cloud gehen, konzipiert. Für Systeme, die langfristig On-Premise bleiben, bietet SAP weiterhin eine abgewandelte Form des Premium Engagements als Herstellersupport an.

Gibt es eine Kombination aus Success Plans und On-Premise-Engagement – etwa wenn ich On-Premise betreibe, aber BTP (Cloud) nutze?

Ja, es gibt die hybride Möglichkeit: Für On-Premise-Systeme bleibt das abgewandelte Premium Engagement bestehen, und für Cloud-Lösungen wie BTP können Success Plans in der Stufe Advanced oder Max gebucht werden. Beides läuft parallel.

Wie ist das Zusammenspiel mit SAP-Partnern, die die Implementierung übernehmen?

SAP tritt mit den Success Plans nicht in Konkurrenz zu Implementierungspartnern. Die Plans umfassen keine Implementierungsleistungen, sondern bieten komplementär den Zugang zur SAP – speziell hinsichtlich Best Practices, Standards, Architektur und spezieller Expertise. Ziel ist Entscheidungstransparenz: Über SAP Support können Kunden auf jeden Experten innerhalb der SAP zugreifen.

Kann ich zwischen den Success-Plan-Modellen wechseln (Upgrade oder Downgrade)?

Ein Wechsel zwischen den Stufen richtet sich an der Laufzeit der Lizenzverträge. Für Details empfiehlt SAP, den direkten Kontakt aufzunehmen.

Kann ich mit dem Max Plan auch lösungsübergreifend Support erhalten?

Ja. Die Premium Services im Max Plan – etwa Transformation Advisory oder Cross-Solution Process Optimization – können End-to-End über Lösungsgrenzen hinweg genutzt werden, auch wenn der Max Plan nur auf einer Lösung liegt.

Sind die Services im Advanced Plan limitiert?

Nein. Die Services stehen unlimitiert zur Verfügung. Sie subskribieren den Plan und können die enthaltenen Leistungen abrufen, ohne einzelne Beratertage buchen zu müssen.

Wie starte ich mit einem Success Plan?

Der Foundational Plan ist bereits aktiv. Für Advanced oder Max wenden Sie sich an Ihren SAP-Ansprechpartner. Der Plan ist ab Tag 1 auf Ihre Lösung abgestimmt und sofort nutzbar – ohne lange Vertragsverhandlungen.

For Public Cloud Define Instruction Keys is available as SSCUI 101046. SAP Public Cloud KBA explicitly names configuration activity and SAP Public Cloud localization guidance also lists Define Instruction Keys under Payment Medium Workbench

entry.title: NEW SAP Master Data Governance MDG100 E-Learning Course !
entry.content:
Unlock the Power of Data with the New SAP Master Data Governance E-Learning Course

In today’s fast-paced business environment, effective data management is the cornerstone of success. SAP is proud to introduce its latest MDG100 e-learning course, SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA, designed to empower professionals like Data Stewards with the skills and knowledge to master data governance and drive business excellence.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/sap-master-data-governance-on-sap-s-4hana

Why Choose SAP Master Data Governance?

SAP Master Data Governance is a critical solution for organizations aiming to ensure data accuracy, consistency, and compliance across their enterprise. This course provides a comprehensive understanding of SAP’s MDG solution strategy, covering essential data domains, processes, and business applications.

What You’ll Learn

This expertly crafted course is structured into 9 units, each tailored to build your expertise step by step:

  • Unit 1: Introducing SAP Master Data Governance
    Gain foundational knowledge of MDG with 4 lessons in just 48 minutes.
  • Unit 2: Distinguishing Master Data Governance for Material
    Dive deeper into material-specific governance with 2 lessons spanning 1 hr and 23 minutes.
  • Unit 3: Describing Master Data Governance for Business Partner, Supplier, Customer
    Explore the intricacies of managing business partner data in 3 lessons over 2 hrs and 33 minutes.
  • Unit 4: Introducing Master Data Governance for Financials and Hierarchy Management
    Understand financial data governance in 1 lesson lasting 1 hr and 8 minutes.
  • Unit 5: Describing Master Data Governance - Mass Changes and Multiple Object Processing
    Learn about mass changes and object processing in 3 lessons totaling 1 hr and 5 minutes.
  • Unit 6: Discussing Master Data Governance - Mass Processing and Consolidation
    Master the art of mass processing and consolidation in 3 lessons over 1 hr and 13 minutes.
  • Unit 7: Describing Process Analytics in SAP Master Data Governance
    Discover how to leverage process analytics in MDG with 2 lessons in 44 minutes.
  • Unit 8: Introducing Master Data Governance - Data Quality Management
    Ensure data quality with 4 lessons in 57 minutes.
  • Unit 9: Describing Master Data Governance - Cloud Ready Mode
    Explore cloud-ready MDG capabilities in 5 lessons lasting 24 minutes.

Learning Outcomes

By completing this course, you will be able to:

  • Present SAP’s MDG solution strategy with confidence.
  • Explain the data domains and processes integral to MDG.
  • Understand and apply business processes within MDG to optimize data governance.

Your Learning Journey

Whether you’re just starting out or looking to deepen your expertise, this course is designed to meet your needs:

  • Building the Basics: Lay the foundation for understanding SAP MDG.
  • Develop Your Expertise: Gain practical insights into MDG processes and applications.
  • Skill Deepening Learning: Enhance your knowledge with advanced concepts.
  • Excel in Your Expertise: Achieve mastery in SAP MDG.
  • Advanced Specialization Learning: Specialize in specific areas of MDG for targeted expertise.

Why Enroll?

This e-learning course is your gateway to becoming a master in data governance, enabling you to drive efficiency, ensure compliance, and unlock the full potential of SAP S/4HANA. Whether you’re an aspiring data professional or a seasoned expert, this course will equip you with the tools to excel in your role.

Start Learning Today!

  • Don’t miss the opportunity to elevate your career and make a strategic move towards mastering data governance. 
  • Enroll in the SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA e-learning course today and take the first step toward becoming a data governance expert. 
  • Empower your organization with the knowledge to transform data into actionable insights.

    The future of data governance starts with you!

entry.title: Re: How to Take SAP’s New Performance-Based Certification Exams

Hi @tracy_kramerEIR66 , thanks for learning with us and sharing your question. @Amin_Omidy  is right, that a detailed score report is on the roadmap and we're working it with priority but cannot share an ETA yet. entry.title: From Raw Data to Data Products: End-to-End Implementation using SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
This is my first blog and I will walks through a practical implementation of how data from SAP S/4HANA can be ingested into the Object Store, transformed into a business-ready Data Product within SAP Datasphere, and securely shared via the BDC Cockpit to Databricks using delta-enabled data sharing. Along the way, we will cover the prerequisites, step-by-step setup, and key considerations to successfully build and share a Delta Share Data Product in SAP Business Data Cloud.

Architecture illustrates how data flows from SAP S/4HANA into the Object Store, is transformed into a Data Product within SAP Datasphere, and is then shared via BDC Cockpit to Databricks using delta-enabled data sharing.

Pre-requisite:

  1. If your SAP Datasphere tenant is part of an SAP Business Data Cloud formation, then you can create data products for SAP Business Data Cloud.
  2. A Data Provider Profile should be created.
  3. An Object Store Space should be created and the required roles should be assigned.

Object Store Space

The Object Store space has been created and deployed in datasphere, and the required roles have been assigned to create replication flows.

Replication Flow

Create a replication flow for the I_PRODUCT table in the Object Store space, with the table load type set to Initial and Delta.

Delta Table

This is generated after the successful execution of the replication flow.

Data Provider Profile

To create a Delta Share data product, you must create a data provider profile with a dedicated visibility option called Formations.

  1. From the Data Sharing Cockpit, go to the My Data Provider Profile app, click Create Data Provider Profile to open the data provider page.
  2. Fill out your profile.
  3. Select the visibility option Formations.
  4. Enter a Namespace. A prefix based on your landscape's name is automatically generated to ensure consistency.
  5. Once you've created your Formations profile, you can create a Delta Share data product.

Creating a Delta Share Data Product

  1. In Data Sharing Cockpit > My Data Products, click Create Data Product.
  2. Enter the Business Name and the mandatory Technical Name of the data product.
  3. Fill out the Product Details.

As you're creating a data product as a Formations provider, the matching context is automatically assigned by default. 

Similarly, the Data Shipment is automatically set by default to Integrated Delivery, and the Delivery Mode to Delta Share. You cannot change these values.

  1. In the Pricing section, the Contract Type is also set by default to Free, and you cannot change it either.

Create Data Product
Select the Object Store Space.

 Context in new Data product will display the data provider name.

Shipment types: Integrated Delivery

The data is copied directly into the space that the consumers select.

Add Product Artifacts
Select the space where your local tables containing the data are included. The Artifact Space is automatically set to SAP HANA DATA LAKE storage type instead of regular HANA Cloud spaces.

Note: The SAP HANA DATA LAKE spaces don't contain any views so you can only add local tables and Only tables with supported column data types are displayed.

The lifecycle state of the new data product is set to Draft. To make it available for consumers, select the data product from the page My Data Products.

Click Switch Status to change the status to "Listed".

Status of the Data Product will be updated to Listed

Catalog users will be able to search for and evaluate these data products.

  • When the catalog is accessed from an SAP Datasphere system, users can install the data product to a space.
  • When the catalog is accessed from an SAP Business Data Cloud system, users can share the data product to a target system.

If at a later time you change the lifecycle status of a data product to Delisted, the data product will not be available in the catalog anymore. If the data product is installed to a space or shared to a target system, users won't be able to uninstall the data product or remove the shares until its status is changed back to Listed. 

Note: You can delete Delta Share data products once they are uninstalled from all SAP Datasphere spaces and are no longer shared with any tenants.

When you delete a Delta Share data product, the system also deletes the corresponding data share tables from SAP HANA DATA LAKE spaces.

Please note that once you've deleted a data product, you cannot create a new one with the same technical name.

SAP Business Data Cloud
Go to BDC Cockpit and search for data product.

Make sure you can share a data product by checking the following statuses:
The data product's release status is Active, its lifecycle status is Active, and its functional status is Current.

Share Data Product

Context:

Please remember that data products can only be shared to different systems within the same formation. You're not allowed to share them to the same system they were created from. If the target system you want to select is the same system that created the data product, use that system's native sharing capabilities instead. 

  1. In the side navigation area, choose (Catalog & Marketplace) > (Search).
  2. On the catalog search page, use the filters or the search to find the data product you want and choose it to open its details page.

In the dialog, choose Add Target to share the data product.

Choose the system you want to share to. If only one target is available, it's automatically selected. Here select Databricks system

Enter a share name
The name that you enter is the name that users will see in the SAP Databricks, and it cannot be changed after the data product is shared. Select the default workspace. You can select to share the data product with up to five workspaces, including the default workspace.

Choose Share
A message appears letting you know that the share process has started. As the share process runs, you'll receive a notification letting you know that the process started and a notification of when it finishes with the result. You'll also see that the Manage Access dialog continually refreshes until the share process finishes.

Databricks
Login to Databricks

Your data product is shared and its share status is updated and visible in the catalog search results. The name that you entered is the name that users will see in SAP Databricks, and it cannot be changed. If you need to change the name, you must delete the share setting for the data product and then share it to the target system again.

In conclusion, SAP Business Data Cloud provides a robust and scalable framework to transform raw data into business-ready insights. By leveraging Object Store for data ingestion, SAP Datasphere for modeling, and seamless data sharing to platforms like Databricks, organizations can unlock the full potential of their data. This approach not only ensures performance and governance but also enables cross-platform analytics in a consistent and efficient manner.

SAP Datasphere SAP Business Data Cloud SAP Databricks Object Store on SAP BTP

entry.title: Replenishment Process - EWM Decentralized
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I’m working on activating a replenishment (REPL) process and I have a question about HU reuse during replenishment.

The business requirement is the following:
If there is already an available HU in a bin, the system should suggest this HU as the destination during replenishment and allow the bin to be completed using the same HU.

Environment

  • System: Decentralized EWM
  • Release: S/4HANA 2021 FPS05 (on‑premise)

Scenario

  • When a bin is empty, the user creates a new HU, which is expected and works fine.
  • After several processes, this HU reaches the minimum quantity.
  • When REPL runs again, the system proposes creating another HU to complete the replenishment.
  • Instead, the requirement is to continue filling the existing HU until it is complete, rather than creating a new one.

Question

Is it possible, using standard EWM configuration, to make the system reuse an existing HU in the bin during replenishment instead of proposing a new HU?

Storage type Config:

WPT config:

entry.title: Which authorizations are not in SAP_ALL profile?
entry.content:
The authorization profile SAP_ALL is said to contain all authorizations but it's in fact not exactly 100% true.

After regenerating the authorization profile SAP_ALL via the program RSUSR046 (*), I see that it's missing the authorization objects S_RFCACL, S_DDLACRUD. Maybe more are missing?

Are there any other concerned authorization objects and is there an official documentation somewhere?

Thanks.

Sandra

(*) For information, the SAP_ALL authorization profile is also generated automatically when you export or import authorization objects (especially custom) via transport requests (objects R3TR SUSO XXXX).

entry.title: Re: Which authorizations are not in SAP_ALL profile?
entry.content:
The program AGR_REGENERATE_SAP_ALL, more specifically its subroutine CREATE_SAP_ALL, contains the "source of truth" of what is included in SAP_ALL and under which conditions:

  • The internal table I_TOBJ is initialized first with all authorization objects, with the following components: OCLSS = authorization class, OBJCT = authorization object.
  • If the table PRGN_CUST doesn't contain the parameter ADD_OLD_AUTH_OBJECTS or it exists and its value is not YES:
    • delete i_tobj where oclss = 'AAAA'.
  • If the table PRGN_CUST contains the parameter ADD_ALL_CUST_OBJECTS and its value is NO:
    • delete i_tobj where objct(1) = 'Y' or objct(1) = 'Z'.
  • Always do:
    • delete I_TOBJ where objct = 'S_DDLACRUD'.
    • NB: S_DDLACRUD is for controlling authorizations when creating, displaying, updating or deleting a CDS Annotation Definition
  • If the table PRGN_CUST doesn't contain the parameter ADD_S_RFCACL or its value is neither YES nor RESTRICTED:
    • delete i_tobj where objct = 'S_RFCACL'.
  • If the table PRGN_CUST contains the parameter ADD_S_RFCACL and its value is RESTRICTED, the authorization object S_RFC_ACL is set with these authorization fields (see the note 2624572):
    • RFC_EQUSER = 'Y'
    • RFC_USER = ' '
    • * for all other fields (ACTVT, RFC_CLIENT, RFC_INFO, RFC_SYSID, RFC_TCODE)

NB: I could find only this documentation about SAP_ALL special authorization objects and I couldn't find anything about S_DDLACRUD:

  • 410424 - Customizing for generation of profile SAP_ALL - SAP for Me
    • Report RSUSR406: Generation of profile SAP_ALL in the current client
    • Report AGR_REGENERATE_SAP_ALL: Generation of profile SAP_ALL in all clients
    • Customizing switches in the table PRGN_CUST:
      • ADD_ALL_CUST_OBJECTS YES (default), NO - include full authorization for customer authorization objects (namespaces Y and Z) in the profile SAP_ALL
      • ADD_OLD_AUTH_OBJECTS NO (default), YES - include full authorization for obsolete authorization objects (class AAAA) in the profile SAP_ALL
      • ADD_S_RFCACL NO (default), YES - include full authorization for S_RFCACL in the profile SAP_ALL (more explanations in the note 2624572)
  • 2624572 - SAP_ALL | Handling of authorization object S_RFCACL - SAP for Me
    • Transaction SM30_PRGN_CUST to maintain the parameter ADD_S_RFCACL 
  • 439753 - Generating SAP_ALL with a transport of authorization objects - SAP for Me
    • Performance improvement: tThe new version of AGR_REGENERATE_SAP_ALL determines the required and the current state and writes only the required changes to the database.
    • Moreover, the parameter 'SAP_ALL_GENERATION' and ATTRIBUTE = 'OFF' may be set to not generate automatically the SAP_ALL profile. The parameter may be changed via the transaction SM30, on the table PRGN_CUST.

entry.title: Re: MIGO - Tornar MKPF-BKTXT obrigatório e preenchimento automático (movimentos 542/ZRT)
entry.content:
Oi,

Esse comportamento é esperado na MIGO e está relacionado ao ciclo de processamento da aplicação.

Apesar da sequência dos métodos parecer ser:
PBO_HEADER → PAI_HEADER → CHECK_HEADER

na prática, a MIGO executa múltiplos ciclos de PBO e PAI antes de chegar no CHECK_HEADER. Com isso, valores atribuídos no PAI_HEADER nem sempre permanecem disponíveis até o momento da validação.

Uma forma de contornar isso é:

  • usar o PAI_HEADER apenas para calcular o valor;
  • armazenar temporariamente (por exemplo, via MEMORY ID (EXPORT));
  • preencher o GOHEAD-BKTXT no PBO_HEADER (para refletir na tela)
    Exemplo: 

 

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <fs_gohead> TYPE gohead.

DATA: lv_bktxt TYPE gohead-bktxt.

IMPORT bktxt = lv_bktxt  FROM MEMORY ID 'ZMIGO_BKTXT'.
  IF lv_bktxt IS NOT INITIAL.
    ASSIGN ('(SAPLMIGO)GOHEAD') TO <fs_gohead>.
    IF <fs_gohead> IS ASSIGNED.
      <fs_gohead>-bktxt = lv_bktxt.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

 

  • e recuperar esse valor no CHECK_HEADER para validação.

Não é a abordagem mais “elegante”, mas funciona bem nesse cenário, já que a MIGO não mantém um estado compartilhado confiável entre os métodos da BAdI.

Talvez isso ajude no seu caso 🙂 Fiz um teste e deu certo.

entry.title: Re: Import of tables T059M and T059T fails: DT617 "Maintain size category for table XXXX"
entry.content:
The solution is described in the note 2809904, although the message described in the symptom is different:

2809904 - SE13 | Fill out all required entry fields - SAP for Me

The note says to change the technical settings of the standard tables via the transaction code SE13:

In field Data Class enter value APPL2
In field Size Category enter value 1

The note lists all the tables concerned: T003M, T003T, ... , T059M, T059T, ... , TZGRT, TZUNT.

---------------

For information, the note 2494250 describes the same symptom for the table T094, but mentions that both messages may occur: "Maintain size category for table T094" (DT617) and "Fill out all required entry fields" (00055).

2494250 - Missing entries in the technical settings of the table T094 - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: RBP Permissions have been affected with the new peopl...
entry.content:
What appears to have happened is over the weekend 1H Release for Preview, SAP has decided to change everything they had us advising customers on regarding a Downloadable Report under General Settings (Configure People Profile). SAP advised to run migration job and update the Latest People Profile Categories with the Downloaded Permission Roles .csv Excel output.  NOW, customers cannot perform this action.  And the 1H migration over the weekend has superseeded the prior instructions.  

Now customers only see the migrated Legacy Profile with the Everyone (default).  DO NOT BE confused by this.  It does not mean that "Everyone" can now see something permission-wise.   The Permission Roles you have are still in place and SHOULD BE RESPECTED by the Latest People Profile.  What we are encountering are additional one-off scenarios, where SAP has apparently altered the Compensation (Edit/View All UI) as well as relocated fields such as Employee Status on Job Info to the Job Info header info.  

I would advise all customers to regression test and check all RBP's accordingly.  As well as verify the Enablement of Visibility check boxes as well as any custom_MDF enabled portlets.  Hope this helps!

entry.title: Understanding SAP Commerce Cloud Alerting and Thresholds
entry.content:
To keep your environments stable, SAP Commerce Cloud uses specific alert thresholds. These alerts help identify performance issues, infrastructure failures, and shifts in user experience.

Here is a breakdown of how these monitoring rules work based on official documentation.

⚙️Performance Alerts and Baselines

Performance thresholds are dynamic. They are calculated using automated baselining based on a rolling window of the last seven days of historical data.

  • Note: Every service can have different thresholds, and they change over time. Customers cannot modify these thresholds.

Service Alerts (Frontend, Backend, Background, and External)

The system monitors response time and failure rates for all infrastructure tiers including:

  • Frontend, e.g www.xxx.com:443
  • Backend, e.g xxxstorefront, cmswebservices
  • Background, e.g Cron job, TaskEngine
  • External services, e.g ‘Requests to public networks’, ‘Requests to unmonitored hosts’

They are defined as follows:

  • Response time: enabled

Property

Setting

Detection mode

Automatic

All requests

o   Absolute threshold: 500 ms

o   Relative threshold: 100%

Slowest 10%

o   Absolute threshold: 1000 ms

o   Relative threshold: 200%

Avoid over-alerting

o   Only alert if there are at least: 10 requests per minute

o   Only alert if the abnormal state remains for at least: 1 minute

 

  • Failure Rate: enabled

Property

Setting

Detection mode

Automatic

Absolute threshold

10%

Relative threshold

100%

Avoid over-alerting

o   Only alert if there are at least: 10 requests per minute

o   Only alert if the abnormal state remains for at least: 1 minute

 

Dynamic threshold rules - Database

The scope for these database alerts (e.g xxx-xxx-p1-db) are as follows:

  • Response time degradation
  • Failure rate increase
  • Failed database connects

The settings for database alerts are defined as follows:

  • Response time: enabled

Property

Setting

Detection mode

Automatic

All requests

o   Absolute threshold: 5 ms

o   Relative threshold: 50%

Slowest 10%

o   Absolute threshold: 20 ms

o   Relative threshold: 100%

Avoid over-alerting

o   Only alert if there are at least: 10 requests per minute

o   Only alert if the abnormal state remains for at least: 1 minute

 

  • Failure Rate: enabled

Property

Setting

Detection mode

Automatic

Absolute threshold

5%

Relative threshold

50%

Avoid over-alerting

o   Only alert if there are at least: 10 requests per minute

o   Only alert if the abnormal state remains for at least: 1 minute

External Services Monitoring

By default, third-party requests are grouped by domain/host/IP under "Requests to public networks" or "Requests to unmonitored hosts.".

To monitor critical third-party services better, SAP has preconfigured all mainstream Payment service providers as standalone services. If you use an unlisted payment service or you need to monitor other business critical third-party services, you can raise a support ticket to mark them as standalone.

 

⚙️Infrastructure Alerts

The scope for these database alerts (e.g xxx-xxx-p1-db) are as follows:

  • Host
    • CPU
    • Memory
    • Disk
    • Network
  • Process Group/Instance
    • CPU
    • Memory
  • Database
    • DTU/CPU
    • Storage

The settings for availability alerts are enabled as follows:

  • Host:

Property

Setting

CPU saturation

Alert if CPU usage is higher than 95% in 3 of 5 one-minute intervals.

Memory event usage

o   Alert if memory usage is higher than 90% on Windows or 80% on Linux

o   Alert if the memory page fault rate is higher than 100 faults/s on Windows or 20 faults/s on Linux in 3 of 5 one-minute intervals

GC activity

o   Alert if GC time is higher than 40%

o   Alert if GC suspension if higher than 25% in 3 of 5 one-minute intervals

Java out of memory

Alert if the number of Java out-of-memory exceptions is 1 per minute or higher

 

  • Network:

Property

Setting

Number of dropped packets

o   Alert if the receive/transmit dropped packet percentage is higher than 10%.

o   AND the total packets rate is higher than 10 packets/s in 3 of 5 one-minute intervals

Network utilization

Alert if the sent/received traffic utilization is higher than 90% in 3 of 5 one-minute intervals

TCP connectivity for process

o   Alert if the percentage of new connection failures is higher than 3%.

o   AND the number of failed connections is higher than 10 connections/min in 3 of 5 one-minute intervals

Retransmission rate

o   Alert if the retransmission rate is higher than 3%.

o   AND the number of retransmitted packets is higher than 10 packets/min in 3 of 5 one-minute intervals

 

  • Disk:

Property

Setting

Low disk space

Alert if the free disk space is lower than 3% in 3 of 5 one-minute intervals

Slow running disks

Alert if the disk read time or write time is higher than 200 ms in 3 of 5 one-minute intervals

Inodes number available

Alert if the percent of available inodes is lower than 5% in 3 of 5 one-minute intervals

⚙️Database Infrastructure Alerts

The scope for these database alerts (e.g xxx-xxx-p1-db) are as follows:

  • Azure DTU/CPU Usage Critical
  • Azure ReadOnly DB DTU/CPU Usage Critical
  • Azure DB Storage Usage

⚙️Digital Experience & Synthetic Monitoring

Application Alerts

The system detects JavaScript errors and performance degradation for user actions. It also monitors Traffic Drops: an alert triggers if observed traffic is less than 50% of the expected value for at least 1 minute.

Synthetic Alerts (Availability)

Synthetic monitors simulate user behavior to check availability:

  • Global Outage: Alert if all locations cannot access the application 1 time.
  • Partial Outage: Alert if at least 1/3 of locations fail 2 times consecutively.
  • Performance: Alert if the total duration of events exceeds 10 seconds.

Unsupported Customization

Please note that the following are not supported:

  • Environment-specific custom alerts.
  • Custom alert thresholds.

Critical Anomaly Conditions

Specific conditions that trigger immediate alerts include:

  • Apache Workers: Pool usage over 99%.
  • Solr Index: 10+ exceptions (e.g., "Sync failed" or "Connection lost") over 5 minutes.
  • Tomcat: Idle threads fall under 2% for four consecutive minutes.
  • Pods: 10 restarts within 15 minutes.

To understand this methodology in depth, see Dynatrace documentation

I hope this overview helps you better understand how monitoring works in your environment. By using automated baselines and predefined thresholds, the system does the heavy lifting for you—catching performance lags and infrastructure glitches before they impact your business.

Use these insights to keep your store fast, stable, and always ready for your customers!

entry.title: Re: Integración de los vales de despensa al salario Diari...
entry.content:
Hola!

La funcionalidad estándar de la nómina mexicana de SAP ofrece una opción para tratar la integración al salario por este tipo de conceptos.

Lo primero es crear un nuevo concepto de nómina y configurarlo con el valor "0" en la Clase de Procesamiento (PC) 62. Como concepto ejemplo puedes considerar el que ahora utilizas para el vale de despensa variable (incluyendo la admisibilidad en el IT0377) o bien, el concepto modelo M330.

Con eso es suficiente para que la funcion del IMSS para los semanales y mensuales cuyo pago se realiza en cada periodo de nómina.

Para los quincenales deberás crear dos conceptos: uno para el recibo con el total que se paga en la 2a quincena del mes; otro para generarlo e integrarlo al salario en cada quincena (con el 50% de lo que pagas al mes). El primero puede ser similar al que ahora utilizas y el segundo sería sólo para efectos técnicos de la integración.

Lo que debes tener mucho cuidado es de que esos pagos realmente sean fijos ya que si varía el importe de un periodo a otro, el cálculo de la nómina intentará generar un nuevo SDI. Si el vale de despensa está sujeto a otros factores como contratación a mitad del periodo, baja a mitad del periodo, ausencias, incapacidades, etc. entonces podrás estar recibiendo mensajes de advertencia si se detecta una diferencia con respecto a la parte fija que integraste en el periodo inmediato anterior.

Por ejemplo: si pagas habitualmente 700 pesos de vale de despensa semanal, entonces, esa cantidad se divide entre 7 para obtener la proporción diaria del pago (100 pesos por día) y se integra lo que exceda del 40% de la UMA diaria. Pero, si por cualquier circunstancia en un periodo en particular, en vez de 700, pagas 600 pesos, entonces se estará dividiendo esa cantidad entre 7 (resutando en 85.71 pesos) y se integrará lo que exceda del mismo tope diario. En este caso, el cálculo te advertirá que el SDI debe recalcularse. Luego, si al siguiente periodo, vuelves a pagar lo acostumbrado, 700 pesos, entonces se volverá a exigir el recálculo del nuevo SDI. Ese es el inconveniente en el tratamiento de conceptos "fijos" de integración. Deben asegurarse que realmente sean fijos.

Deben de poner especial atención en la generación del concepto /WD1 ya que ese concepto contiene la cantidad de días del periodo con el cual se dividirán los elementos fijos de integración para obtener la proporción diaria. Si en el ejemplo anterior, para el periodo donde pagaron los 600 pesos de vale, se genera el concepto /WD1 con 6 días, entonces no tendrán ninguna diferencia en el SDI.

 

Espero esto te sea de utilidad.

entry.title: Port to port (PP) or Port to Door (PD) for sales sea freight booking (SFB) scenarios.
entry.content:
Hello SAP experts,
I am working with SAP Transportation Management (TM) in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and have a question regarding the automatic determination of movement types for Sea Freight Bookings (SFB).
Currently, when creating an SFB from a Sales Order via the Freight Unit (FU), the system automatically determines movement types such as Door to Port (DP) or Door to Door (DD). This behavior seems driven by the Incoterms, IncLoc1 ID, IncLoc2 ID, and shipping point fields in the Sales Order.

I am trying to achieve the following:
Is it possible to have the system automatically create the SFB with Movement Type Port to Port (PP) or Port to Door (PD)?
Observation on Manual Creation: If I create the SFB manually, select the PP or PD movement type, and then manually assign the Freight Unit, the system automatically determines the Loading Port as the Shipping Point. Is this the standard expected behavior? Field Determination: Is there a specific field I might be missing in the Sales Order that would explicitly trigger a "Port" as the starting location instead of the Shipping Point?

 

 


Current Workaround:
A viable solution I have found is to create the SFB manually, select PP or PD, add the FU, and then manually insert an intermediate stage to correctly define the Loading Port. However, the goal is to have the Loading Port correctly identified during SFB creation without adding extra stages.
Is this workaround the only valid solution in the Public Edition, or is there a way to influence the initial stage building and movement type determination directly from the predecessor document?

 

Thank you for your insights!
